parts.text: Let's review @.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml this github action, what's the purpose and how the code is well structured and reliable.
Called the Read tool with the following input: {"filePath":"/Users/pnodet/git/nivalis/biome-config/.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml"}

<path>/Users/pnodet/git/nivalis/biome-config/.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: name: Biome Insights Artifact
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       config-repository:
7:         description: Repository that hosts normalizer scripts.
8:         required: false
9:         type: string
10:         default: nivalis-studio/biome-config
11:       config-ref:
12:         description: Ref in config repository.
13:         required: false
14:         type: string
15:         default: main
16:       shared-config-version:
17:         description: Shared config version/hash used by caller repo.
18:         required: false
19:         type: string
20:         default: ''
21:       local-config-hash:
22:         description: Optional local config hash for drift tracking.
23:         required: false
24:         type: string
25:         default: ''
26: 
27: permissions:
28:   contents: read
29:   issues: write
30:   pull-requests: write
31: 
32: jobs:
33:   artifact:
34:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
35:     steps:
36:       - name: Checkout caller repository
37:         uses: actions/checkout@v6
38: 
39:       - name: Setup Node
40:         uses: actions/setup-node@v6
41:         with:
42:           node-version-file: '.node-version'
43: 
44:       - name: Setup Biome
45:         uses: biomejs/setup-biome@v2
46: 
47:       - name: Setup pnpm
48:         uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
49: 
50:       - name: Install dependencies
51:         run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
52: 
53:       - name: Run Biome CI JSON reporter
54:         id: biome
55:         run: |
56:           set +e
57:           mkdir -p .biome-insights
58:           biome ci --reporter=json . > "$RUNNER_TEMP/biome-raw.json"
59:           BIOME_EXIT=$?
60:           echo "exit_code=$BIOME_EXIT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
61:           exit 0
62: 
63:       - name: Checkout biome-config scripts
64:         uses: actions/checkout@v6
65:         with:
66:           repository: ${{ inputs.config-repository }}
67:           ref: ${{ inputs.config-ref }}
68:           path: .nivalis-biome-config
69: 
70:       - name: Normalize diagnostics artifact
71:         env:
72:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || '' }}
73:           LOCAL_CONFIG_HASH: ${{ inputs.local-config-hash }}
74:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number || '' }}
75:           SHARED_CONFIG_VERSION: ${{ inputs.shared-config-version }}
76:         run: |
77:           BIOME_VERSION=$(biome --version)
78:           node .nivalis-biome-config/scripts/biome-insights/normalize.mjs \
79:             --input "$RUNNER_TEMP/biome-raw.json" \
80:             --output .biome-insights/biome-normalized.json \
81:             --repo "${{ github.repository }}" \
82:             --pr "$PR_NUMBER" \
83:             --sha "${{ github.sha }}" \
84:             --base-sha "$BASE_SHA" \
85:             --run-id "${{ github.run_id }}" \
86:             --timestamp "${{ github.run_started_at }}" \
87:             --biome-version "$BIOME_VERSION" \
88:             --shared-config-version "$SHARED_CONFIG_VERSION" \
89:             --local-config-hash "$LOCAL_CONFIG_HASH"
90: 
91:       - name: Upload normalized artifact
92:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
93:         with:
94:           name: biome-insights-${{ github.repository_id }}-${{ github.run_id }}
95:           path: .biome-insights/
96:           if-no-files-found: error
97: 
98:       - name: Upsert PR summary comment for touched-file errors
99:         if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
100:         continue-on-error: true
101:         uses: actions/github-script@v7
102:         with:
103:           script: |
104:             const fs = require('node:fs');
105:             const path = require('node:path');
106: 
107:             const marker = '<!-- nivalis-biome-insights:changed-files-errors:v1 -->';
108:             const maxRows = 50;
109: 
110:             const normalizePath = value => {
111:               return String(value ?? '')
112:                 .replaceAll('\\', '/')
113:                 .replace(/^\.\//, '')
114:                 .replace(/^\//, '');
115:             };
116: 
117:             const artifactPath = path.resolve('.biome-insights/biome-normalized.json');
118: 
119:             if (!fs.existsSync(artifactPath)) {
120:               core.warning(`No normalized artifact found at ${artifactPath}.`);
121:               return;
122:             }
123: 
124:             const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(artifactPath, 'utf8'));
125:             const diagnostics = Array.isArray(parsed?.diagnostics)
126:               ? parsed.diagnostics
127:               : [];
128: 
129:             const pullNumber = context.payload.pull_request?.number;
130: 
131:             if (!pullNumber) {
132:               core.info('No pull request context found. Skipping comment.');
133:               return;
134:             }
135: 
136:             const changedFiles = await github.paginate(github.rest.pulls.listFiles, {
137:               owner: context.repo.owner,
138:               repo: context.repo.repo,
139:               pull_number: pullNumber,
140:               per_page: 100,
141:             });
142: 
143:             const touchedPaths = new Set();
144: 
145:             for (const file of changedFiles) {
146:               if (file.status !== 'removed') {
147:                 touchedPaths.add(normalizePath(file.filename));
148:               }
149: 
150:               if (file.status === 'renamed' && file.previous_filename) {
151:                 touchedPaths.add(normalizePath(file.previous_filename));
152:               }
153:             }
154: 
155:             const touchedErrors = diagnostics
156:               .filter(diagnostic => {
157:                 return (
158:                   String(diagnostic?.severity).toLowerCase() === 'error' &&
159:                   touchedPaths.has(normalizePath(diagnostic?.filePath))
160:                 );
161:               })
162:               .sort((left, right) => {
163:                 const leftPath = normalizePath(left.filePath);
164:                 const rightPath = normalizePath(right.filePath);
165: 
166:                 if (leftPath !== rightPath) {
167:                   return leftPath.localeCompare(rightPath);
168:                 }
169: 
170:                 const leftLine = Number(left.line ?? 0);
171:                 const rightLine = Number(right.line ?? 0);
172: 
173:                 if (leftLine !== rightLine) {
174:                   return leftLine - rightLine;
175:                 }
176: 
177:                 const leftRule = `${left.domain}/${left.rule}`;
178:                 const rightRule = `${right.domain}/${right.rule}`;
179: 
180:                 if (leftRule !== rightRule) {
181:                   return leftRule.localeCompare(rightRule);
182:                 }
183: 
184:                 return String(left.fingerprint ?? '').localeCompare(
185:                   String(right.fingerprint ?? ''),
186:                 );
187:               });
188: 
189:             const filesWithErrors = new Set(
190:               touchedErrors.map(diagnostic => normalizePath(diagnostic.filePath)),
191:             );
192: 
193:             const displayRows = touchedErrors.slice(0, maxRows);
194:             const hiddenCount = touchedErrors.length - displayRows.length;
195: 
196:             const formatRule = diagnostic => {
197:               return `${diagnostic.domain}/${diagnostic.rule}`;
198:             };
199: 
200:             const formatMessage = diagnostic => {
201:               return String(diagnostic.message ?? '')
202:                 .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
203:                 .trim();
204:             };
205: 
206:             const lines = [
207:               marker,
208:               '## Biome summary (changed files)',
209:               '',
210:               `- Errors on changed files: **${touchedErrors.length}**`,
211:               `- Changed files with errors: **${filesWithErrors.size}**`,
212:               '',
213:             ];
214: 
215:             if (touchedErrors.length === 0) {
216:               lines.push('No Biome errors found on touched files in this PR.');
217:             } else {
218:               lines.push('| File | Line | Rule | Message |');
219:               lines.push('| --- | --- | --- | --- |');
220: 
221:               for (const diagnostic of displayRows) {
222:                 const filePath = normalizePath(diagnostic.filePath);
223:                 const line = Number(diagnostic.line ?? 1);
224:                 const rule = formatRule(diagnostic);
225:                 const message = formatMessage(diagnostic)
226:                   .replaceAll('|', '\\|')
227:                   .slice(0, 160);
228:                 lines.push(`| \`${filePath}\` | ${line} | \`${rule}\` | ${message} |`);
229:               }
230: 
231:               if (hiddenCount > 0) {
232:                 lines.push('');
233:                 lines.push(`Showing first ${maxRows} findings. ${hiddenCount} additional findings are omitted.`);
234:               }
235:             }
236: 
237:             lines.push('');
238:             lines.push('_This comment is updated on each workflow run._');
239: 
240:             const body = lines.join('\n');
241:             const comments = await github.paginate(github.rest.issues.listComments, {
242:               owner: context.repo.owner,
243:               repo: context.repo.repo,
244:               issue_number: pullNumber,
245:               per_page: 100,
246:             });
247: 
248:             const existingComment = comments.find(comment => {
249:               return comment.body?.includes(marker);
250:             });
251: 
252:             if (existingComment) {
253:               await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
254:                 owner: context.repo.owner,
255:                 repo: context.repo.repo,
256:                 comment_id: existingComment.id,
257:                 body,
258:               });
259: 
260:               core.info(`Updated PR comment ${existingComment.id}.`);
261:               return;
262:             }
263: 
264:             await github.rest.issues.createComment({
265:               owner: context.repo.owner,
266:               repo: context.repo.repo,
267:               issue_number: pullNumber,
268:               body,
269:             });
270: 
271:             core.info('Created PR comment.');
272: 
273:       - name: Fail when Biome reports diagnostics
274:         if: steps.biome.outputs.exit_code != '0'
275:         run: exit 1

(End of file - total 275 lines)
</content>

state.output:
<skill_content name="requesting-code-review">
# Skill: requesting-code-review

# Requesting Code Review

Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent to catch issues before they cascade. The reviewer gets precisely crafted context for evaluation — never your session's history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the work product, not your thought process, and preserves your own context for continued work.

**Core principle:** Review early, review often.

## When to Request Review

**Mandatory:**
- After each task in subagent-driven development
- After completing major feature
- Before merge to main

**Optional but valuable:**
- When stuck (fresh perspective)
- Before refactoring (baseline check)
- After fixing complex bug

## How to Request

**1. Get git SHAs:**
```bash
BASE_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD~1)  # or origin/main
HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
```

**2. Dispatch code-reviewer subagent:**

Use Task tool with superpowers:code-reviewer type, fill template at `code-reviewer.md`

**Placeholders:**
- `{WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED}` - What you just built
- `{PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS}` - What it should do
- `{BASE_SHA}` - Starting commit
- `{HEAD_SHA}` - Ending commit
- `{DESCRIPTION}` - Brief summary

**3. Act on feedback:**
- Fix Critical issues immediately
- Fix Important issues before proceeding
- Note Minor issues for later
- Push back if reviewer is wrong (with reasoning)

## Example

```
[Just completed Task 2: Add verification function]

You: Let me request code review before proceeding.

BASE_SHA=$(git log --oneline | grep "Task 1" | head -1 | awk '{print $1}')
HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

[Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent]
  WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: Verification and repair functions for conversation index
  PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task 2 from docs/superpowers/plans/deployment-plan.md
  BASE_SHA: a7981ec
  HEAD_SHA: 3df7661
  DESCRIPTION: Added verifyIndex() and repairIndex() with 4 issue types

[Subagent returns]:
  Strengths: Clean architecture, real tests
  Issues:
    Important: Missing progress indicators
    Minor: Magic number (100) for reporting interval
  Assessment: Ready to proceed

You: [Fix progress indicators]
[Continue to Task 3]
```

## Integration with Workflows

**Subagent-Driven Development:**
- Review after EACH task
- Catch issues before they compound
- Fix before moving to next task

**Executing Plans:**
- Review after each batch (3 tasks)
- Get feedback, apply, continue

**Ad-Hoc Development:**
- Review before merge
- Review when stuck

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Skip review because "it's simple"
- Ignore Critical issues
- Proceed with unfixed Important issues
- Argue with valid technical feedback

**If reviewer wrong:**
- Push back with technical reasoning
- Show code/tests that prove it works
- Request clarification

See template at: requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md

Base directory for this skill: file:///Users/pnodet/.cache/opencode/node_modules/superpowers/skills/requesting-code-review
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>
<file>/Users/pnodet/.cache/opencode/node_modules/superpowers/skills/requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md</file>
</skill_files>
</skill_content>

parts.text:
Great workflow overall — it’s designed as a reusable “collect + normalize + surface + enforce” Biome quality gate.

- Its core purpose is to run `biome ci`, normalize raw diagnostics into a stable JSON artifact, upload that artifact, and still fail the run when Biome finds issues (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:53`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:70`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:91`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:273`).
- Because it’s triggered via `workflow_call`, other repos can reuse it consistently and pass version/hash metadata for drift tracking (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:4`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:16`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:21`).

What is well structured

- Clear phase ordering: setup → run Biome → normalize → upload artifact → PR comment → final fail gate. Easy to reason about and debug.
- Smart control flow: Biome step captures exit code but exits `0` so downstream artifact/comment steps still run (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:56`-`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:61`), then enforcement happens at the end (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:273`).
- Good separation of concerns: normalization logic is centralized in shared script rather than embedded shell/YAML logic (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:78`).
- Least-privilege-ish permissions are explicit and scoped (`contents: read`, `issues/pull-requests: write`) (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:27`).
- PR comment logic is robust: marker-based upsert, pagination, path normalization, renamed file handling, deterministic sorting, truncation limits (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:107`-`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:271`).

Reliability assessment

- Reliable by design for developer feedback loops: even failing lint still yields artifact + PR summary.
- Non-critical comment posting is `continue-on-error: true`, so API hiccups won’t block the build (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:100`).
- `if-no-files-found: error` protects against silent artifact upload failures (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:96`).

Main risks / improvements I’d consider

1. Pin GitHub Actions by commit SHA (not only major tags) for stronger supply-chain safety (`actions/checkout@v6`, etc.).
2. Add `timeout-minutes` at job level to avoid hangs.
3. Consider Node fallback if `.node-version` is missing in some caller repos (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:42`).
4. Optionally set `if: always()` on artifact upload if you want artifacts even when normalization unexpectedly fails (tradeoff: may upload partial results).

If you want, I can propose a tightened version of this workflow with SHA pinning + timeout + a small reliability hardening patch.

Because it’s metadata, not an execution dependency.

- `shared-config-version` is only passed into normalization as context (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:16`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:75`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:88`).
- The actual workflow behavior (run Biome, produce artifact, fail on diagnostics) does not depend on it (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:53`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:70`, `.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:273`).
- Making it optional with `default: ''` keeps callers backward-compatible and reusable across repos that may not consume a shared config version yet (`.github/workflows/biome-insights-artifact.yaml:20`).
- It also avoids forcing every caller workflow to wire an input that may be unavailable in some contexts (older repos, local-only config, nonstandard setups).

So: it’s optional by design to improve adoption and compatibility, while still collecting the value when provided.
